qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unlimited TCP queries per connection enable denial-of-service via resource exhaustion (MaxTCPQueries=-1)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns CoreDNS TCP and DNS-over-TLS listeners setting MaxTCPQueries=-1 in core/dnsserver/server.go and server_tls.go. The advisory concerns the DoH GET /dns-query path in plugin/pkg/doh/doh.go accepting oversized dns= query parameters before early size validation, URL query parsing, base64 decoding, and message unpacking.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a CoreDNS TCP/DoT MaxTCPQueries limit issue, while GHSA-63cw-r7xf-jmwr is caused by missing early size validation on the DoH GET dns= query parameter before URL query parsing and base64 decoding in plugin/pkg/doh/doh.go.
evidence: Judge manifest lists unlimited_tcp_queries_dos as the only final qualifying finding.

unlimited_tcp_queries_dos reports code locations core/dnsserver/server.go:443, core/dnsserver/server.go:162, core/dnsserver/server_tls.go:107, and core/dnsserver/server_tls.go:58 for MaxTCPQueries=-1.
The advisory identifies plugin/pkg/doh/doh.go requestToMsgGet() and base64ToMsg() as the vulnerable DoH GET path handling oversized dns= query values before validation.
